description_long: This is a hand tool with a robust wooden handle and a metallic head featuring two distinct ends: a broad, flat blade on one side and a pointed pick on the other. The handle is cylindrical, straight, and appears to be made of varnished wood, providing a comfortable grip for manual use. The metallic head is securely mounted atop the handle, with the blade and pick extending horizontally in opposite directions. The blade is wedge-shaped, suitable for chopping or splitting, while the pick end is tapered to a sharp point, ideal for piercing or prying. The bottom of the handle is capped with a metal spike, enhancing its utility for puncturing or anchoring. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding.
description: A sturdy wooden-handled axe with a dual-ended metal head—one side a wedge blade, the other a pointed pick—and a metal spike at the handle's base.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from a side angle, clearly displaying the wedge-shaped blade, pointed pick, and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: A direct end-on view, showing the narrow profile of the axe head and the cylindrical handle, with both the top and bottom metal points aligned.
description_view_2: The opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the blade and pick visible but reversed in orientation.
description_view_3: A rear end-on view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the alignment of the handle and the symmetry of the head's attachment.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, smooth finish, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Dual-ended head with a wedge-shaped blade on one side and a pointed pick on the other; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the metal head fitted onto the wooden handle.
